began on April 19, 1948. The regular season ended on October 4, with the [Boston Braves](/wiki/Boston_Braves "Boston Braves") and [Cleveland Indians](/wiki/Cleveland_Guardians "Cleveland Guardians") as the regular season champions of the [National League](/wiki/National_League_(baseball) "National League (baseball)") and [American League](/wiki/American_League "American League"), respectively. The Indians won the American League title via a [tie-breaker game](/wiki/1948_American_League_tie-breaker_game "1948 American League tie-breaker game") victory over the [Boston Red Sox](/wiki/Boston_Red_Sox "Boston Red Sox"), after both teams finished their 154-game schedules with identical 96–58 records. This was the second regular season tie-breaker, and saw a change from the [previous three-game format](/wiki/1946_National_League_tie-breaker_series "1946 National League tie-breaker series") to that of a single-game, [Game 163](/wiki/List_of_Major_League_Baseball_tie-breakers "List of Major League Baseball tie-breakers"). The postseason began with Game 1 of the [45th World Series](/wiki/1948_World_Series "1948 World Series") on October 6 and ended with Game 6 on October 11. The Indians defeated the Braves, four games to two, capturing their second championship in franchise history, since their previous in [1920](/wiki/1920_Major_League_Baseball_season "1920 Major League Baseball season"). Going into the season, the defending [World Series](/wiki/World_Series "World Series") champions were the [New York Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees") from the [1947](/wiki/1947_Major_League_Baseball_season "1947 Major League Baseball season") season.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c1/ButtonBlue.svg/20px-ButtonBlue.svg.png)](/wiki/File:ButtonBlue.svg) [National League](/wiki/National_League_(baseball) "National League (baseball)")

The **1948 major league baseball season** began on April 19, 1948. The regular season ended on October 4, with the [Boston Braves](/wiki/Boston_Braves "Boston Braves") and [Cleveland Indians](/wiki/Cleveland_Guardians "Cleveland Guardians") as the regular season champions of the [National League](/wiki/National_League_(baseball) "National League (baseball)") and [American League](/wiki/American_League "American League"), respectively. The Indians won the American League title via a [tie-breaker game](/wiki/1948_American_League_tie-breaker_game "1948 American League tie-breaker game") victory over the [Boston Red Sox](/wiki/Boston_Red_Sox "Boston Red Sox"), after both teams finished their 154-game schedules with identical 96–58 records. This was the second regular season tie-breaker, and saw a change from the [previous three-game format](/wiki/1946_National_League_tie-breaker_series "1946 National League tie-breaker series") to that of a single-game, [Game 163](/wiki/List_of_Major_League_Baseball_tie-breakers "List of Major League Baseball tie-breakers"). The postseason began with Game 1 of the [45th World Series](/wiki/1948_World_Series "1948 World Series") on October 6 and ended with Game 6 on October 11. The Indians defeated the Braves, four games to two, capturing their second championship in franchise history, since their previous in [1920](/wiki/1920_Major_League_Baseball_season "1920 Major League Baseball season"). Going into the season, the defending [World Series](/wiki/World_Series "World Series") champions were the [New York Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees") from the [1947](/wiki/1947_Major_League_Baseball_season "1947 Major League Baseball season") season.

The [15th Major League Baseball All-Star Game](/wiki/1948_Major_League_Baseball_All-Star_Game "1948 Major League Baseball All-Star Game") was held on July 13 at [Sportsman's Park](/wiki/Sportsman%27s_Park "Sportsman's Park") in [St. Louis](/wiki/St._Louis "St. Louis"), [Missouri](/wiki/Missouri "Missouri"), hosted by the [St. Louis Browns](/wiki/St._Louis_Browns "St. Louis Browns"). The [American League](/wiki/American_League "American League") won, 5–2, for their third straight win.

The 1948 season is notable as being the final season of any [Negro League](/wiki/Negro_league_baseball "Negro league baseball") holding major-league status, as per MLB's 2020 designation of Negro Leagues. It would be the final season of the [Negro National League](/wiki/Negro_National_League_(1933%E2%80%931948) "Negro National League (1933–1948)"), while the [Negro American League](/wiki/Negro_American_League "Negro American League") would continue as a minor-league until it ceased play after the [1962](/wiki/1962_in_baseball "1962 in baseball") season.

## Schedule

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1948_Major_League_Baseball_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Schedule")]

See also: [Major League Baseball schedule](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball_schedule "Major League Baseball schedule")

The 1948 schedule consisted of 154 games for all teams in the American League and National League, each of which had eight teams. Each team was scheduled to play 22 games against the other seven teams of their respective league. This continued the format put in place since the [1904](/wiki/1904_Major_League_Baseball_season "1904 Major League Baseball season") season (except for [1919](/wiki/1919_Major_League_Baseball_season "1919 Major League Baseball season")) and would be used until [1961](/wiki/1961_Major_League_Baseball_season "1961 Major League Baseball season") in the American League and [1962](/wiki/1962_Major_League_Baseball_season "1962 Major League Baseball season") in the National League.

Opening Day took place on April 19, featuring six teams. The final day of the scheduled regular season was on October 3, which saw all sixteen teams play, continuing the trend from [1946](/wiki/1946_Major_League_Baseball_season "1946 Major League Baseball season"). Due to the [Boston Red Sox](/wiki/Boston_Red_Sox "Boston Red Sox") and [Cleveland Indians](/wiki/Cleveland_Guardians "Cleveland Guardians") finishing with the same record of 96–58, a [tie-breaker game](/wiki/1948_American_League_tie-breaker_game "1948 American League tie-breaker game") was scheduled, to be considered an extension of the regular season. The Indians won the [Game 163](/wiki/List_of_Major_League_Baseball_tie-breakers "List of Major League Baseball tie-breakers") tie-breaker on October 4. The [World Series](/wiki/1948_World_Series "1948 World Series") took place between October 6 and October 11.

## Retired numbers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1948_Major_League_Baseball_season&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Retired numbers")]

* [Babe Ruth](/wiki/Babe_Ruth "Babe Ruth") had his No. 3 retired by the [New York Yankees](/wiki/New_York_Yankees "New York Yankees") on June 13. This was the second number retired by the team.
* [Mel Ott](/wiki/Mel_Ott "Mel Ott") had his No. 4 retired by the [New York Giants](/wiki/New_York_Giants_(baseball) "New York Giants (baseball)") on July 17. This was the second number retired by the team.
